55: func (b *backend) pathLogin() *framework.Path {
56: 	return &framework.Path{
57: 		Pattern: "login$",
58: 		Fields: map[string]*framework.FieldSchema{
59: 			"role": {
60: 				Type: framework.TypeString,
61: 				Description: `Name of the role against which the login is being attempted.
62: If 'role' is not specified, then the login endpoint looks for a role
63: bearing the name of the AMI ID of the EC2 instance that is trying to login.
64: If a matching role is not found, login fails.`,
65: 			},
66: 
67: 			"pkcs7": {
68: 				Type: framework.TypeString,
69: 				Description: `PKCS7 signature of the identity document when using an auth_type
70: of ec2.`,
71: 			},
72: 
73: 			"nonce": {
74: 				Type: framework.TypeString,
75: 				Description: `The nonce to be used for subsequent login requests when
76: auth_type is ec2.  If this parameter is not specified at
77: all and if reauthentication is allowed, then the backend will generate a random
78: nonce, attaches it to the instance's identity access list entry and returns the
79: nonce back as part of auth metadata.  This value should be used with further
80: login requests, to establish client authenticity. Clients can choose to set a
81: custom nonce if preferred, in which case, it is recommended that clients provide
82: a strong nonce.  If a nonce is provided but with an empty value, it indicates
83: intent to disable reauthentication. Note that, when 'disallow_reauthentication'
84: option is enabled on either the role or the role tag, the 'nonce' holds no
85: significance.`,
86: 			},
87: 
88: 			"iam_http_request_method": {
89: 				Type: framework.TypeString,
90: 				Description: `HTTP method to use for the AWS request when auth_type is
91: iam. This must match what has been signed in the
92: presigned request. Currently, POST is the only supported value`,
93: 			},
94: 
95: 			"iam_request_url": {
96: 				Type: framework.TypeString,
97: 				Description: `Base64-encoded full URL against which to make the AWS request
98: when using iam auth_type.`,
99: 			},
100: 
101: 			"iam_request_body": {
102: 				Type: framework.TypeString,
103: 				Description: `Base64-encoded request body when auth_type is iam.
104: This must match the request body included in the signature.`,
105: 			},
106: 			"iam_request_headers": {
107: 				Type: framework.TypeHeader,
108: 				Description: `Key/value pairs of headers for use in the
109: sts:GetCallerIdentity HTTP requests headers when auth_type is iam. Can be either 
110: a Base64-encoded, JSON-serialized string, or a JSON object of key/value pairs. 
111: This must at a minimum include the headers over which AWS has included a  signature.`,
112: 			},
113: 			"identity": {
114: 				Type: framework.TypeString,
115: 				Description: `Base64 encoded EC2 instance identity document. This needs to be supplied along
116: with the 'signature' parameter. If using 'curl' for fetching the identity
117: document, consider using the option '-w 0' while piping the output to 'base64'
118: binary.`,
119: 			},
120: 			"signature": {
121: 				Type: framework.TypeString,
122: 				Description: `Base64 encoded SHA256 RSA signature of the instance identity document. This
123: needs to be supplied along with 'identity' parameter.`,
124: 			},
125: 		},
126: 
127: 		Operations: map[logical.Operation]framework.OperationHandler{
128: 			logical.UpdateOperation: &framework.PathOperation{
129: 				Callback: b.pathLoginUpdate,
130: 			},
131: 			logical.AliasLookaheadOperation: &framework.PathOperation{
132: 				Callback: b.pathLoginUpdate,
133: 			},
134: 			logical.ResolveRoleOperation: &framework.PathOperation{
135: 				Callback: b.pathLoginResolveRole,
136: 			},
137: 		},
138: 
139: 		HelpSynopsis:    pathLoginSyn,
140: 		HelpDescription: pathLoginDesc,
141: 	}
142: }

144: func (b *backend) pathLoginResolveRole(ctx context.Context, req *logical.Request, data *framework.FieldData) (*logical.Response, error) {
145: 	anyEc2, allEc2 := hasValuesForEc2Auth(data)
146: 	anyIam, allIam := hasValuesForIamAuth(data)
147: 	switch {
148: 	case anyEc2 && anyIam:
149: 		return logical.ErrorResponse("supplied auth values for both ec2 and iam auth types"), nil
150: 	case anyEc2 && !allEc2:
151: 		return logical.ErrorResponse("supplied some of the auth values for the ec2 auth type but not all"), nil
152: 	case anyEc2:
153: 		return b.pathLoginResolveRoleEc2(ctx, req, data)
154: 	case anyIam && !allIam:
155: 		return logical.ErrorResponse("supplied some of the auth values for the iam auth type but not all"), nil
156: 	case anyIam:
157: 		return b.pathLoginResolveRoleIam(ctx, req, data)
158: 	default:
159: 		return logical.ErrorResponse("didn't supply required authentication values"), nil
160: 	}
161: }

163: func (b *backend) pathLoginEc2GetRoleNameAndIdentityDoc(ctx context.Context, req *logical.Request, data *framework.FieldData) (string, *identityDocument, *logical.Response, error) {
164: 	identityDocB64 := data.Get("identity").(string)
165: 	var identityDocBytes []byte
166: 	var err error
167: 	if identityDocB64 != "" {
168: 		identityDocBytes, err = base64.StdEncoding.DecodeString(identityDocB64)
169: 		if err != nil || len(identityDocBytes) == 0 {
170: 			return "", nil, logical.ErrorResponse("failed to base64 decode the instance identity document"), nil
171: 		}
172: 	}
173: 
174: 	signatureB64 := data.Get("signature").(string)
175: 	var signatureBytes []byte
176: 	if signatureB64 != "" {
177: 		signatureBytes, err = base64.StdEncoding.DecodeString(signatureB64)
178: 		if err != nil {
179: 			return "", nil, logical.ErrorResponse("failed to base64 decode the SHA256 RSA signature of the instance identity document"), nil
180: 		}
181: 	}
182: 
183: 	pkcs7B64 := data.Get("pkcs7").(string)
184: 
185: 	// Either the pkcs7 signature of the instance identity document, or
186: 	// the identity document itself along with its SHA256 RSA signature
187: 	// needs to be provided.
188: 	if pkcs7B64 == "" && (len(identityDocBytes) == 0 && len(signatureBytes) == 0) {
189: 		return "", nil, logical.ErrorResponse("either pkcs7 or a tuple containing the instance identity document and its SHA256 RSA signature needs to be provided"), nil
190: 	} else if pkcs7B64 != "" && (len(identityDocBytes) != 0 && len(signatureBytes) != 0) {
191: 		return "", nil, logical.ErrorResponse("both pkcs7 and a tuple containing the instance identity document and its SHA256 RSA signature is supplied; provide only one"), nil
192: 	}
193: 
194: 	// Verify the signature of the identity document and unmarshal it
195: 	var identityDocParsed *identityDocument
196: 	if pkcs7B64 != "" {
197: 		identityDocParsed, err = b.parseIdentityDocument(ctx, req.Storage, pkcs7B64)
198: 		if err != nil {
199: 			return "", nil, nil, err
200: 		}
201: 		if identityDocParsed == nil {
202: 			return "", nil, logical.ErrorResponse("failed to verify the instance identity document using pkcs7"), nil
203: 		}
204: 	} else {
205: 		identityDocParsed, err = b.verifyInstanceIdentitySignature(ctx, req.Storage, identityDocBytes, signatureBytes)
206: 		if err != nil {
207: 			return "", nil, nil, err
208: 		}
209: 		if identityDocParsed == nil {
210: 			return "", nil, logical.ErrorResponse("failed to verify the instance identity document using the SHA256 RSA digest"), nil
211: 		}
212: 	}
213: 
214: 	roleName := data.Get("role").(string)
215: 
216: 	// If roleName is not supplied, a role in the name of the instance's AMI ID will be looked for
217: 	if roleName == "" {
218: 		roleName = identityDocParsed.AmiID
219: 	}
220: 
221: 	// Get the entry for the role used by the instance
222: 	// Note that we don't return the roleEntry, but use it to determine if the role exists
223: 	// roleEntry does not contain the role name, so it is not appropriate to return
224: 	roleEntry, err := b.role(ctx, req.Storage, roleName)
225: 	if err != nil {
226: 		return "", nil, nil, err
227: 	}
228: 	if roleEntry == nil {
229: 		return "", nil, logical.ErrorResponse(fmt.Sprintf("entry for role %q not found", roleName)), nil
230: 	}
231: 	return roleName, identityDocParsed, nil, nil
232: }

242: func (b *backend) pathLoginIamGetRoleNameCallerIdAndEntity(ctx context.Context, req *logical.Request, data *framework.FieldData) (string, *GetCallerIdentityResult, *iamEntity, *logical.Response, error) {
243: 	method := data.Get("iam_http_request_method").(string)
244: 	if method == "" {
245: 		return "", nil, nil, logical.ErrorResponse("missing iam_http_request_method"), nil
246: 	}
247: 
248: 	// In the future, might consider supporting GET
249: 	if method != "POST" {
250: 		return "", nil, nil, logical.ErrorResponse("invalid iam_http_request_method; currently only 'POST' is supported"), nil
251: 	}
252: 
253: 	rawUrlB64 := data.Get("iam_request_url").(string)
254: 	if rawUrlB64 == "" {
255: 		return "", nil, nil, logical.ErrorResponse("missing iam_request_url"), nil
256: 	}
257: 	rawUrl, err := base64.StdEncoding.DecodeString(rawUrlB64)
258: 	if err != nil {
259: 		return "", nil, nil, logical.ErrorResponse("failed to base64 decode iam_request_url"), nil
260: 	}
261: 	parsedUrl, err := url.Parse(string(rawUrl))
262: 	if err != nil {
263: 		return "", nil, nil, logical.ErrorResponse("error parsing iam_request_url"), nil
264: 	}
265: 	if parsedUrl.RawQuery != "" {
266: 		// Should be no query parameters
267: 		return "", nil, nil, logical.ErrorResponse(logical.ErrInvalidRequest.Error()), nil
268: 	}
269: 	// TODO: There are two potentially valid cases we're not yet supporting that would
270: 	// necessitate this check being changed. First, if we support GET requests.
271: 	// Second if we support presigned POST requests
272: 	bodyB64 := data.Get("iam_request_body").(string)
273: 	if bodyB64 == "" {
274: 		return "", nil, nil, logical.ErrorResponse("missing iam_request_body"), nil
275: 	}
276: 	bodyRaw, err := base64.StdEncoding.DecodeString(bodyB64)
277: 	if err != nil {
278: 		return "", nil, nil, logical.ErrorResponse("failed to base64 decode iam_request_body"), nil
279: 	}
280: 	body := string(bodyRaw)
281: 	if err = validateLoginIamRequestBody(body); err != nil {
282: 		return "", nil, nil, logical.ErrorResponse(err.Error()), nil
283: 	}
284: 
285: 	headers := data.Get("iam_request_headers").(http.Header)
286: 	if len(headers) == 0 {
287: 		return "", nil, nil, logical.ErrorResponse("missing iam_request_headers"), nil
288: 	}
289: 
290: 	config, err := b.lockedClientConfigEntry(ctx, req.Storage)
291: 	if err != nil {
292: 		return "", nil, nil, logical.ErrorResponse("error getting configuration"), nil
293: 	}
294: 
295: 	endpoint := "https://sts.amazonaws.com"
296: 
297: 	maxRetries := awsClient.DefaultRetryerMaxNumRetries
298: 	if config != nil {
299: 		if config.IAMServerIdHeaderValue != "" {
300: 			err = validateVaultHeaderValue(headers, parsedUrl, config.IAMServerIdHeaderValue)
301: 			if err != nil {
302: 				return "", nil, nil, logical.ErrorResponse(fmt.Sprintf("error validating %s header: %v", iamServerIdHeader, err)), nil
303: 			}
304: 		}
305: 		if err = config.validateAllowedSTSHeaderValues(headers); err != nil {
306: 			return "", nil, nil, logical.ErrorResponse(err.Error()), nil
307: 		}
308: 		if config.STSEndpoint != "" {
309: 			endpoint = config.STSEndpoint
310: 		}
311: 		if config.MaxRetries >= 0 {
312: 			maxRetries = config.MaxRetries
313: 		}
314: 	}
315: 
316: 	callerID, err := submitCallerIdentityRequest(ctx, maxRetries, method, endpoint, parsedUrl, body, headers)
317: 	if err != nil {
318: 		return "", nil, nil, logical.ErrorResponse(fmt.Sprintf("error making upstream request: %v", err)), nil
319: 	}
320: 
321: 	entity, err := parseIamArn(callerID.Arn)
322: 	if err != nil {
323: 		return "", nil, nil, logical.ErrorResponse(fmt.Sprintf("error parsing arn %q: %v", callerID.Arn, err)), nil
324: 	}
325: 
326: 	roleName := data.Get("role").(string)
327: 	if roleName == "" {
328: 		roleName = entity.FriendlyName
329: 	}
330: 	return roleName, callerID, entity, nil, nil
331: }

341: // instanceIamRoleARN fetches the IAM role ARN associated with the given
342: // instance profile name
343: func (b *backend) instanceIamRoleARN(iamClient *iam.IAM, instanceProfileName string) (string, error) {
344: 	if iamClient == nil {
345: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("nil iamClient")
346: 	}
347: 	if instanceProfileName == "" {
348: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("missing instance profile name")
349: 	}
350: 
351: 	profile, err := iamClient.GetInstanceProfile(&iam.GetInstanceProfileInput{
352: 		InstanceProfileName: aws.String(instanceProfileName),
353: 	})
354: 	if err != nil {
355: 		return "", awsutil.AppendAWSError(err)
356: 	}
357: 	if profile == nil {
358: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("nil output while getting instance profile details")
359: 	}
360: 
361: 	if profile.InstanceProfile == nil {
362: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("nil instance profile in the output of instance profile details")
363: 	}
364: 
365: 	if profile.InstanceProfile.Roles == nil || len(profile.InstanceProfile.Roles) != 1 {
366: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("invalid roles in the output of instance profile details")
367: 	}
368: 
369: 	if profile.InstanceProfile.Roles[0].Arn == nil {
370: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("nil role ARN in the output of instance profile details")
371: 	}
372: 
373: 	return *profile.InstanceProfile.Roles[0].Arn, nil
374: }

376: // validateInstance queries the status of the EC2 instance using AWS EC2 API
377: // and checks if the instance is running and is healthy
378: func (b *backend) validateInstance(ctx context.Context, s logical.Storage, instanceID, region, accountID string) (*ec2.Instance, error) {
379: 	// Create an EC2 client to pull the instance information
380: 	ec2Client, err := b.clientEC2(ctx, s, region, accountID)
381: 	if err != nil {
382: 		return nil, err
383: 	}
384: 
385: 	status, err := ec2Client.DescribeInstances(&ec2.DescribeInstancesInput{
386: 		InstanceIds: []*string{
387: 			aws.String(instanceID),
388: 		},
389: 	})
390: 	if err != nil {
391: 		errW := fmt.Errorf("error fetching description for instance ID %q: %w", instanceID, err)
392: 		return nil, errwrap.Wrap(errW, awsutil.CheckAWSError(err))
393: 	}
394: 	if status == nil {
395: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("nil output from describe instances")
396: 	}
397: 	if len(status.Reservations) == 0 {
398: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("no reservations found in instance description")
399: 	}
400: 	if len(status.Reservations[0].Instances) == 0 {
401: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("no instance details found in reservations")
402: 	}
403: 	if *status.Reservations[0].Instances[0].InstanceId != instanceID {
404: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("expected instance ID not matching the instance ID in the instance description")
405: 	}
406: 	if status.Reservations[0].Instances[0].State == nil {
407: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("instance state in instance description is nil")
408: 	}
409: 	if *status.Reservations[0].Instances[0].State.Name != "running" {
410: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("instance is not in 'running' state")
411: 	}
412: 	return status.Reservations[0].Instances[0], nil
413: }

415: // validateMetadata matches the given client nonce and pending time with the
416: // one cached in the identity access list during the previous login. But, if
417: // reauthentication is disabled, login attempt is failed immediately.
418: func validateMetadata(clientNonce, pendingTime string, storedIdentity *accessListIdentity, roleEntry *awsRoleEntry) error {
419: 	// For sanity
420: 	if !storedIdentity.DisallowReauthentication && storedIdentity.ClientNonce == "" {
421: 		return fmt.Errorf("client nonce missing in stored identity")
422: 	}
423: 
424: 	// If reauthentication is disabled or if the nonce supplied matches a
425: 	// predefined nonce which indicates reauthentication to be disabled,
426: 	// authentication will not succeed.
427: 	if storedIdentity.DisallowReauthentication ||
428: 		subtle.ConstantTimeCompare([]byte(reauthenticationDisabledNonce), []byte(clientNonce)) == 1 {
429: 		return fmt.Errorf("reauthentication is disabled")
430: 	}
431: 
432: 	givenPendingTime, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339, pendingTime)
433: 	if err != nil {
434: 		return err
435: 	}
436: 
437: 	storedPendingTime, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339, storedIdentity.PendingTime)
438: 	if err != nil {
439: 		return err
440: 	}
441: 
442: 	// When the presented client nonce does not match the cached entry, it
443: 	// is either that a rogue client is trying to login or that a valid
444: 	// client suffered a migration. The migration is detected via
445: 	// pendingTime in the instance metadata, which sadly is only updated
446: 	// when an instance is stopped and started but *not* when the instance
447: 	// is rebooted. If reboot survivability is needed, either
448: 	// instrumentation to delete the instance ID from the access list is
449: 	// necessary, or the client must durably store the nonce.
450: 	//
451: 	// If the `allow_instance_migration` property of the registered role is
452: 	// enabled, then the client nonce mismatch is ignored, as long as the
453: 	// pending time in the presented instance identity document is newer
454: 	// than the cached pending time. The new pendingTime is stored and used
455: 	// for future checks.
456: 	//
457: 	// This is a weak criterion and hence the `allow_instance_migration`
458: 	// option should be used with caution.
459: 	if subtle.ConstantTimeCompare([]byte(clientNonce), []byte(storedIdentity.ClientNonce)) != 1 {
460: 		if !roleEntry.AllowInstanceMigration {
461: 			return fmt.Errorf("client nonce mismatch")
462: 		}
463: 		if roleEntry.AllowInstanceMigration && !givenPendingTime.After(storedPendingTime) {
464: 			return fmt.Errorf("client nonce mismatch and instance meta-data incorrect")
465: 		}
466: 	}
467: 
468: 	// Ensure that the 'pendingTime' on the given identity document is not
469: 	// before the 'pendingTime' that was used for previous login. This
470: 	// disallows old metadata documents from being used to perform login.
471: 	if givenPendingTime.Before(storedPendingTime) {
472: 		return fmt.Errorf("instance meta-data is older than the one used for previous login")
473: 	}
474: 	return nil
475: }

477: // Verifies the integrity of the instance identity document using its SHA256
478: // RSA signature. After verification, returns the unmarshaled instance identity
479: // document.
480: func (b *backend) verifyInstanceIdentitySignature(ctx context.Context, s logical.Storage, identityBytes, signatureBytes []byte) (*identityDocument, error) {
481: 	if len(identityBytes) == 0 {
482: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("missing instance identity document")
483: 	}
484: 
485: 	if len(signatureBytes) == 0 {
486: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("missing SHA256 RSA signature of the instance identity document")
487: 	}
488: 
489: 	// Get the public certificates that are used to verify the signature.
490: 	// This returns a slice of certificates containing the default
491: 	// certificate and all the registered certificates via
492: 	// 'config/certificate/<cert_name>' endpoint, for verifying the RSA
493: 	// digest.
494: 	publicCerts, err := b.awsPublicCertificates(ctx, s, false)
495: 	if err != nil {
496: 		return nil, err
497: 	}
498: 	if publicCerts == nil || len(publicCerts) == 0 {
499: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("certificates to verify the signature are not found")
500: 	}
501: 
502: 	// Check if any of the certs registered at the backend can verify the
503: 	// signature
504: 	for _, cert := range publicCerts {
505: 		err := cert.CheckSignature(x509.SHA256WithRSA, identityBytes, signatureBytes)
506: 		if err == nil {
507: 			var identityDoc identityDocument
508: 			if decErr := jsonutil.DecodeJSON(identityBytes, &identityDoc); decErr != nil {
509: 				return nil, decErr
510: 			}
511: 			return &identityDoc, nil
512: 		}
513: 	}
514: 
515: 	return nil, fmt.Errorf("instance identity verification using SHA256 RSA signature is unsuccessful")
516: }

518: // Verifies the correctness of the authenticated attributes present in the PKCS#7
519: // signature. After verification, extracts the instance identity document from the
520: // signature, parses it and returns it.
521: func (b *backend) parseIdentityDocument(ctx context.Context, s logical.Storage, pkcs7B64 string) (*identityDocument, error) {
522: 	// Insert the header and footer for the signature to be able to pem decode it
523: 	pkcs7B64 = fmt.Sprintf("-----BEGIN PKCS7-----\n%s\n-----END PKCS7-----", pkcs7B64)
524: 
525: 	// Decode the PEM encoded signature
526: 	pkcs7BER, pkcs7Rest := pem.Decode([]byte(pkcs7B64))
527: 	if len(pkcs7Rest) != 0 {
528: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to decode the PEM encoded PKCS#7 signature")
529: 	}
530: 
531: 	// Parse the signature from asn1 format into a struct
532: 	pkcs7Data, err := pkcs7.Parse(pkcs7BER.Bytes)
533: 	if err != nil {
534: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to parse the BER encoded PKCS#7 signature: %w", err)
535: 	}
536: 
537: 	// Get the public certificates that are used to verify the signature.
538: 	// This returns a slice of certificates containing the default certificate
539: 	// and all the registered certificates via 'config/certificate/<cert_name>' endpoint
540: 	publicCerts, err := b.awsPublicCertificates(ctx, s, true)
541: 	if err != nil {
542: 		return nil, err
543: 	}
544: 	if publicCerts == nil || len(publicCerts) == 0 {
545: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("certificates to verify the signature are not found")
546: 	}
547: 
548: 	// Before calling Verify() on the PKCS#7 struct, set the certificates to be used
549: 	// to verify the contents in the signer information.
550: 	pkcs7Data.Certificates = publicCerts
551: 
552: 	// Verify extracts the authenticated attributes in the PKCS#7 signature, and verifies
553: 	// the authenticity of the content using 'dsa.PublicKey' embedded in the public certificate.
554: 	if err := pkcs7Data.Verify(); err != nil {
555: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to verify the signature: %w", err)
556: 	}
557: 
558: 	// Check if the signature has content inside of it
559: 	if len(pkcs7Data.Content) == 0 {
560: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("instance identity document could not be found in the signature")
561: 	}
562: 
563: 	var identityDoc identityDocument
564: 	if err := jsonutil.DecodeJSON(pkcs7Data.Content, &identityDoc); err != nil {
565: 		return nil, err
566: 	}
567: 
568: 	return &identityDoc, nil
569: }

571: func (b *backend) pathLoginUpdate(ctx context.Context, req *logical.Request, data *framework.FieldData) (*logical.Response, error) {
572: 	anyEc2, allEc2 := hasValuesForEc2Auth(data)
573: 	anyIam, allIam := hasValuesForIamAuth(data)
574: 	switch {
575: 	case anyEc2 && anyIam:
576: 		return logical.ErrorResponse("supplied auth values for both ec2 and iam auth types"), nil
577: 	case anyEc2 && !allEc2:
578: 		return logical.ErrorResponse("supplied some of the auth values for the ec2 auth type but not all"), nil
579: 	case anyEc2:
580: 		return b.pathLoginUpdateEc2(ctx, req, data)
581: 	case anyIam && !allIam:
582: 		return logical.ErrorResponse("supplied some of the auth values for the iam auth type but not all"), nil
583: 	case anyIam:
584: 		return b.pathLoginUpdateIam(ctx, req, data)
585: 	default:
586: 		return logical.ErrorResponse("didn't supply required authentication values"), nil
587: 	}
588: }

590: // Returns whether the EC2 instance meets the requirements of the particular
591: // AWS role entry.
592: // The first error return value is whether there's some sort of validation
593: // error that means the instance doesn't meet the role requirements
594: // The second error return value indicates whether there's an error in even
595: // trying to validate those requirements
596: func (b *backend) verifyInstanceMeetsRoleRequirements(ctx context.Context,
597: 	s logical.Storage, instance *ec2.Instance, roleEntry *awsRoleEntry, roleName string, identityDoc *identityDocument) (error, error,
598: ) {
599: 	switch {
600: 	case instance == nil:
601: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("nil instance")
602: 	case roleEntry == nil:
603: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("nil roleEntry")
604: 	case identityDoc == nil:
605: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("nil identityDoc")
606: 	}
607: 
608: 	// Verify that the instance ID matches one of the ones set by the role
609: 	if len(roleEntry.BoundEc2InstanceIDs) > 0 && !strutil.StrListContains(roleEntry.BoundEc2InstanceIDs, *instance.InstanceId) {
610: 		return fmt.Errorf("instance ID %q does not belong to the role %q", *instance.InstanceId, roleName), nil
611: 	}
612: 
613: 	// Verify that the AccountID of the instance trying to login matches the
614: 	// AccountID specified as a constraint on role
615: 	if len(roleEntry.BoundAccountIDs) > 0 && !strutil.StrListContains(roleEntry.BoundAccountIDs, identityDoc.AccountID) {
616: 		return fmt.Errorf("account ID %q does not belong to role %q", identityDoc.AccountID, roleName), nil
617: 	}
618: 
619: 	// Verify that the AMI ID of the instance trying to login matches the
620: 	// AMI ID specified as a constraint on the role.
621: 	//
622: 	// Here, we're making a tradeoff and pulling the AMI ID out of the EC2
623: 	// API rather than the signed instance identity doc. They *should* match.
624: 	// This means we require an EC2 API call to retrieve the AMI ID, but we're
625: 	// already calling the API to validate the Instance ID anyway, so it shouldn't
626: 	// matter. The benefit is that we have the exact same code whether auth_type
627: 	// is ec2 or iam.
628: 	if len(roleEntry.BoundAmiIDs) > 0 {
629: 		if instance.ImageId == nil {
630: 			return nil, fmt.Errorf("AMI ID in the instance description is nil")
631: 		}
632: 		if !strutil.StrListContains(roleEntry.BoundAmiIDs, *instance.ImageId) {
633: 			return fmt.Errorf("AMI ID %q does not belong to role %q", *instance.ImageId, roleName), nil
634: 		}
635: 	}
636: 
637: 	// Validate the SubnetID if corresponding bound was set on the role
638: 	if len(roleEntry.BoundSubnetIDs) > 0 {
639: 		if instance.SubnetId == nil {
640: 			return nil, fmt.Errorf("subnet ID in the instance description is nil")
641: 		}
642: 		if !strutil.StrListContains(roleEntry.BoundSubnetIDs, *instance.SubnetId) {
643: 			return fmt.Errorf("subnet ID %q does not satisfy the constraint on role %q", *instance.SubnetId, roleName), nil
644: 		}
645: 	}
646: 
647: 	// Validate the VpcID if corresponding bound was set on the role
648: 	if len(roleEntry.BoundVpcIDs) > 0 {
649: 		if instance.VpcId == nil {
650: 			return nil, fmt.Errorf("VPC ID in the instance description is nil")
651: 		}
652: 		if !strutil.StrListContains(roleEntry.BoundVpcIDs, *instance.VpcId) {
653: 			return fmt.Errorf("VPC ID %q does not satisfy the constraint on role %q", *instance.VpcId, roleName), nil
654: 		}
655: 	}
656: 
657: 	// Check if the IAM instance profile ARN of the instance trying to
658: 	// login, matches the IAM instance profile ARN specified as a constraint
659: 	// on the role
660: 	if len(roleEntry.BoundIamInstanceProfileARNs) > 0 {
661: 		if instance.IamInstanceProfile == nil {
662: 			return nil, fmt.Errorf("IAM instance profile in the instance description is nil")
663: 		}
664: 		if instance.IamInstanceProfile.Arn == nil {
665: 			return nil, fmt.Errorf("IAM instance profile ARN in the instance description is nil")
666: 		}
667: 		iamInstanceProfileARN := *instance.IamInstanceProfile.Arn
668: 		matchesInstanceProfile := false
669: 		// NOTE: Can't use strutil.StrListContainsGlob. A * is a perfectly valid character in the "path" component
670: 		// of an ARN. See, e.g., https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/APIReference/API_CreateInstanceProfile.html :
671: 		// The path allows strings "containing any ASCII character from the ! (\u0021) thru the DEL character
672: 		// (\u007F), including most punctuation characters, digits, and upper and lowercased letters."
673: 		// So, e.g., arn:aws:iam::123456789012:instance-profile/Some*Path/MyProfileName is a perfectly valid instance
674: 		// profile ARN, and it wouldn't be correct to expand the * in the middle as a wildcard.
675: 		// If a user wants to match an IAM instance profile arn beginning with arn:aws:iam::123456789012:instance-profile/foo*
676: 		// then bound_iam_instance_profile_arn would need to be arn:aws:iam::123456789012:instance-profile/foo**
677: 		// Wanting to exactly match an ARN that has a * at the end is not a valid use case. The * is only valid in the
678: 		// path; it's not valid in the name. That means no valid ARN can ever end with a *. For example,
679: 		// arn:aws:iam::123456789012:instance-profile/Foo* is NOT valid as an instance profile ARN, so no valid instance
680: 		// profile ARN could ever equal that value.
681: 		for _, boundInstanceProfileARN := range roleEntry.BoundIamInstanceProfileARNs {
682: 			switch {
683: 			case strings.HasSuffix(boundInstanceProfileARN, "*") && strings.HasPrefix(iamInstanceProfileARN, boundInstanceProfileARN[:len(boundInstanceProfileARN)-1]):
684: 				matchesInstanceProfile = true
685: 				break
686: 			case iamInstanceProfileARN == boundInstanceProfileARN:
687: 				matchesInstanceProfile = true
688: 				break
689: 			}
690: 		}
691: 		if !matchesInstanceProfile {
692: 			return fmt.Errorf("IAM instance profile ARN %q does not satisfy the constraint role %q", iamInstanceProfileARN, roleName), nil
693: 		}
694: 	}
695: 
696: 	// Check if the IAM role ARN of the instance trying to login, matches
697: 	// the IAM role ARN specified as a constraint on the role.
698: 	if len(roleEntry.BoundIamRoleARNs) > 0 {
699: 		if instance.IamInstanceProfile == nil {
700: 			return nil, fmt.Errorf("IAM instance profile in the instance description is nil")
701: 		}
702: 		if instance.IamInstanceProfile.Arn == nil {
703: 			return nil, fmt.Errorf("IAM instance profile ARN in the instance description is nil")
704: 		}
705: 
706: 		// Fetch the instance profile ARN from the instance description
707: 		iamInstanceProfileARN := *instance.IamInstanceProfile.Arn
708: 
709: 		if iamInstanceProfileARN == "" {
710: 			return nil, fmt.Errorf("IAM instance profile ARN in the instance description is empty")
711: 		}
712: 
713: 		// Extract out the instance profile name from the instance
714: 		// profile ARN
715: 		iamInstanceProfileEntity, err := parseIamArn(iamInstanceProfileARN)
716: 		if err != nil {
717: 			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to parse IAM instance profile ARN %q: %w", iamInstanceProfileARN, err)
718: 		}
719: 
720: 		// Use instance profile ARN to fetch the associated role ARN
721: 		iamClient, err := b.clientIAM(ctx, s, identityDoc.Region, identityDoc.AccountID)
722: 		if err != nil {
723: 			return nil, fmt.Errorf("could not fetch IAM client: %w", err)
724: 		} else if iamClient == nil {
725: 			return nil, fmt.Errorf("received a nil iamClient")
726: 		}
727: 		iamRoleARN, err := b.instanceIamRoleARN(iamClient, iamInstanceProfileEntity.FriendlyName)
728: 		if err != nil {
729: 			return nil, fmt.Errorf("IAM role ARN could not be fetched: %w", err)
730: 		}
731: 		if iamRoleARN == "" {
732: 			return nil, fmt.Errorf("IAM role ARN could not be fetched")
733: 		}
734: 
735: 		matchesInstanceRoleARN := false
736: 		for _, boundIamRoleARN := range roleEntry.BoundIamRoleARNs {
737: 			switch {
738: 			// as with boundInstanceProfileARN, can't use strutil.StrListContainsGlob because * can validly exist in the middle of an ARN
739: 			case strings.HasSuffix(boundIamRoleARN, "*") && strings.HasPrefix(iamRoleARN, boundIamRoleARN[:len(boundIamRoleARN)-1]):
740: 				matchesInstanceRoleARN = true
741: 				break
742: 			case iamRoleARN == boundIamRoleARN:
743: 				matchesInstanceRoleARN = true
744: 				break
745: 			}
746: 		}
747: 		if !matchesInstanceRoleARN {
748: 			return fmt.Errorf("IAM role ARN %q does not satisfy the constraint role %q", iamRoleARN, roleName), nil
749: 		}
750: 	}
751: 
752: 	return nil, nil
753: }

755: // pathLoginUpdateEc2 is used to create a Vault token by the EC2 instances
756: // by providing the pkcs7 signature of the instance identity document
757: // and a client created nonce. Client nonce is optional if 'disallow_reauthentication'
758: // option is enabled on the registered role.
759: func (b *backend) pathLoginUpdateEc2(ctx context.Context, req *logical.Request, data *framework.FieldData) (*logical.Response, error) {
760: 	roleName, identityDocParsed, errResp, err := b.pathLoginEc2GetRoleNameAndIdentityDoc(ctx, req, data)
761: 	if errResp != nil || err != nil {
762: 		return errResp, err
763: 	}
764: 
765: 	// Get the entry for the role used by the instance
766: 	roleEntry, err := b.role(ctx, req.Storage, roleName)
767: 	if err != nil {
768: 		return nil, err
769: 	}
770: 	if roleEntry == nil {
771: 		return logical.ErrorResponse(fmt.Sprintf("entry for role %q not found", roleName)), nil
772: 	}
773: 
774: 	// Check for a CIDR match.
775: 	if len(roleEntry.TokenBoundCIDRs) > 0 {
776: 		if req.Connection == nil {
777: 			b.Logger().Warn("token bound CIDRs found but no connection information available for validation")
778: 			return nil, logical.ErrPermissionDenied
779: 		}
780: 		if !cidrutil.RemoteAddrIsOk(req.Connection.RemoteAddr, roleEntry.TokenBoundCIDRs) {
781: 			return nil, logical.ErrPermissionDenied
782: 		}
783: 	}
784: 
785: 	if roleEntry.AuthType != ec2AuthType {
786: 		return logical.ErrorResponse(fmt.Sprintf("auth method ec2 not allowed for role %s", roleName)), nil
787: 	}
788: 
789: 	identityConfigEntry, err := identityConfigEntry(ctx, req.Storage)
790: 	if err != nil {
791: 		return nil, err
792: 	}
793: 
794: 	identityAlias := ""
795: 
796: 	switch identityConfigEntry.EC2Alias {
797: 	case identityAliasRoleID:
798: 		identityAlias = roleEntry.RoleID
799: 	case identityAliasEC2InstanceID:
800: 		identityAlias = identityDocParsed.InstanceID
801: 	case identityAliasEC2ImageID:
802: 		identityAlias = identityDocParsed.AmiID
803: 	}
804: 
805: 	// If we're just looking up for MFA, return the Alias info
806: 	if req.Operation == logical.AliasLookaheadOperation {
807: 		return &logical.Response{
808: 			Auth: &logical.Auth{
809: 				Alias: &logical.Alias{
810: 					Name: identityAlias,
811: 				},
812: 			},
813: 		}, nil
814: 	}
815: 
816: 	// Validate the instance ID by making a call to AWS EC2 DescribeInstances API
817: 	// and fetching the instance description. Validation succeeds only if the
818: 	// instance is in 'running' state.
819: 	instance, err := b.validateInstance(ctx, req.Storage, identityDocParsed.InstanceID, identityDocParsed.Region, identityDocParsed.AccountID)
820: 	if err != nil {
821: 		return logical.ErrorResponse(fmt.Sprintf("failed to verify instance ID: %v", err)), nil
822: 	}
823: 
824: 	// Verify that the `Region` of the instance trying to login matches the
825: 	// `Region` specified as a constraint on role
826: 	if len(roleEntry.BoundRegions) > 0 && !strutil.StrListContains(roleEntry.BoundRegions, identityDocParsed.Region) {
827: 		return logical.ErrorResponse(fmt.Sprintf("Region %q does not satisfy the constraint on role %q", identityDocParsed.Region, roleName)), nil
828: 	}
829: 
830: 	validationError, err := b.verifyInstanceMeetsRoleRequirements(ctx, req.Storage, instance, roleEntry, roleName, identityDocParsed)
831: 	if err != nil {
832: 		return nil, err
833: 	}
834: 	if validationError != nil {
835: 		return logical.ErrorResponse(fmt.Sprintf("Error validating instance: %v", validationError)), nil
836: 	}
837: 
838: 	// Get the entry from the identity access list, if there is one
839: 	storedIdentity, err := accessListIdentityEntry(ctx, req.Storage, identityDocParsed.InstanceID)
840: 	if err != nil {
841: 		return nil, err
842: 	}
843: 
844: 	// disallowReauthentication value that gets cached at the stored
845: 	// identity access list entry is determined not just by the role entry.
846: 	// If client explicitly sets nonce to be empty, it implies intent to
847: 	// disable reauthentication. Also, role tag can override the 'false'
848: 	// value with 'true' (the other way around is not allowed).
849: 
850: 	// Read the value from the role entry
851: 	disallowReauthentication := roleEntry.DisallowReauthentication
852: 
853: 	clientNonce := ""
854: 
855: 	// Check if the nonce is supplied by the client
856: 	clientNonceRaw, clientNonceSupplied := data.GetOk("nonce")
857: 	if clientNonceSupplied {
858: 		clientNonce = clientNonceRaw.(string)
859: 
860: 		// Nonce explicitly set to empty implies intent to disable
861: 		// reauthentication by the client. Set a predefined nonce which
862: 		// indicates reauthentication being disabled.
863: 		if clientNonce == "" {
864: 			clientNonce = reauthenticationDisabledNonce
865: 
866: 			// Ensure that the intent lands in the access list
867: 			disallowReauthentication = true
868: 		}
869: 	}
870: 
871: 	// This is NOT a first login attempt from the client
872: 	if storedIdentity != nil {
873: 		// Check if the client nonce match the cached nonce and if the pending time
874: 		// of the identity document is not before the pending time of the document
875: 		// with which previous login was made. If 'allow_instance_migration' is
876: 		// enabled on the registered role, client nonce requirement is relaxed.
877: 		if err = validateMetadata(clientNonce, identityDocParsed.PendingTime, storedIdentity, roleEntry); err != nil {
878: 			return logical.ErrorResponse(err.Error()), nil
879: 		}
880: 
881: 		// Don't let subsequent login attempts to bypass the initial
882: 		// intent of disabling reauthentication, despite the properties
883: 		// of role getting updated. For example: Role has the value set
884: 		// to 'false', a role-tag login sets the value to 'true', then
885: 		// role gets updated to not use a role-tag, and a login attempt
886: 		// is made with role's value set to 'false'. Removing the entry
887: 		// from the identity access list should be the only way to be
888: 		// able to login from the instance again.
889: 		disallowReauthentication = disallowReauthentication || storedIdentity.DisallowReauthentication
890: 	}
891: 
892: 	// If we reach this point without erroring and if the client nonce was
893: 	// not supplied, a first time login is implied and that the client
894: 	// intends that the nonce be generated by the backend. Create a random
895: 	// nonce to be associated for the instance ID.
896: 	if !clientNonceSupplied {
897: 		if clientNonce, err = uuid.GenerateUUID(); err != nil {
898: 			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to generate random nonce")
899: 		}
900: 	}
901: 
902: 	// Load the current values for max TTL and policies from the role entry,
903: 	// before checking for overriding max TTL in the role tag.  The shortest
904: 	// max TTL is used to cap the token TTL; the longest max TTL is used to
905: 	// make the access list entry as long as possible as it controls for replay
906: 	// attacks.
907: 	shortestMaxTTL := b.System().MaxLeaseTTL()
908: 	longestMaxTTL := b.System().MaxLeaseTTL()
909: 	if roleEntry.TokenMaxTTL > time.Duration(0) && roleEntry.TokenMaxTTL < shortestMaxTTL {
910: 		shortestMaxTTL = roleEntry.TokenMaxTTL
911: 	}
912: 	if roleEntry.TokenMaxTTL > longestMaxTTL {
913: 		longestMaxTTL = roleEntry.TokenMaxTTL
914: 	}
915: 
916: 	policies := roleEntry.TokenPolicies
917: 	rTagMaxTTL := time.Duration(0)
918: 	var roleTagResp *roleTagLoginResponse
919: 	if roleEntry.RoleTag != "" {
920: 		roleTagResp, err = b.handleRoleTagLogin(ctx, req.Storage, roleName, roleEntry, instance)
921: 		if err != nil {
922: 			return nil, err
923: 		}
924: 		if roleTagResp == nil {
925: 			return logical.ErrorResponse("failed to fetch and verify the role tag"), nil
926: 		}
927: 	}
928: 
929: 	if roleTagResp != nil {
930: 		// Role tag is enabled on the role.
931: 
932: 		// Overwrite the policies with the ones returned from processing the role tag
933: 		// If there are no policies on the role tag, policies on the role are inherited.
934: 		// If policies on role tag are set, by this point, it is verified that it is a subset of the
935: 		// policies on the role. So, apply only those.
936: 		if len(roleTagResp.Policies) != 0 {
937: 			policies = roleTagResp.Policies
938: 		}
939: 
940: 		// If roleEntry had disallowReauthentication set to 'true', do not reset it
941: 		// to 'false' based on role tag having it not set. But, if role tag had it set,
942: 		// be sure to override the value.
943: 		if !disallowReauthentication {
944: 			disallowReauthentication = roleTagResp.DisallowReauthentication
945: 		}
946: 
947: 		// Cache the value of role tag's max_ttl value
948: 		rTagMaxTTL = roleTagResp.MaxTTL
949: 
950: 		// Scope the shortestMaxTTL to the value set on the role tag
951: 		if roleTagResp.MaxTTL > time.Duration(0) && roleTagResp.MaxTTL < shortestMaxTTL {
952: 			shortestMaxTTL = roleTagResp.MaxTTL
953: 		}
954: 		if roleTagResp.MaxTTL > longestMaxTTL {
955: 			longestMaxTTL = roleTagResp.MaxTTL
956: 		}
957: 	}
958: 
959: 	// Save the login attempt in the identity access list
960: 	currentTime := time.Now()
961: 	if storedIdentity == nil {
962: 		// Role, ClientNonce and CreationTime of the identity entry,
963: 		// once set, should never change.
964: 		storedIdentity = &accessListIdentity{
965: 			Role:         roleName,
966: 			ClientNonce:  clientNonce,
967: 			CreationTime: currentTime,
968: 		}
969: 	}
970: 
971: 	// DisallowReauthentication, PendingTime, LastUpdatedTime and
972: 	// ExpirationTime may change.
973: 	storedIdentity.LastUpdatedTime = currentTime
974: 	storedIdentity.ExpirationTime = currentTime.Add(longestMaxTTL)
975: 	storedIdentity.PendingTime = identityDocParsed.PendingTime
976: 	storedIdentity.DisallowReauthentication = disallowReauthentication
977: 
978: 	// Don't cache the nonce if DisallowReauthentication is set
979: 	if storedIdentity.DisallowReauthentication {
980: 		storedIdentity.ClientNonce = ""
981: 	}
982: 
983: 	// Sanitize the nonce to a reasonable length
984: 	if len(clientNonce) > 128 && !storedIdentity.DisallowReauthentication {
985: 		return logical.ErrorResponse("client nonce exceeding the limit of 128 characters"), nil
986: 	}
987: 
988: 	if err = setAccessListIdentityEntry(ctx, req.Storage, identityDocParsed.InstanceID, storedIdentity); err != nil {
989: 		return nil, err
990: 	}
991: 
992: 	auth := &logical.Auth{
993: 		Metadata: map[string]string{
994: 			"role_tag_max_ttl": rTagMaxTTL.String(),
995: 			"role":             roleName,
996: 		},
997: 		Alias: &logical.Alias{
998: 			Name: identityAlias,
999: 		},
1000: 		InternalData: map[string]interface{}{
1001: 			"instance_id": identityDocParsed.InstanceID,
1002: 			"region":      identityDocParsed.Region,
1003: 			"account_id":  identityDocParsed.AccountID,
1004: 		},
1005: 	}
1006: 	roleEntry.PopulateTokenAuth(auth)
1007: 	if err := identityConfigEntry.EC2AuthMetadataHandler.PopulateDesiredMetadata(auth, map[string]string{
1008: 		"instance_id": identityDocParsed.InstanceID,
1009: 		"region":      identityDocParsed.Region,
1010: 		"account_id":  identityDocParsed.AccountID,
1011: 		"ami_id":      identityDocParsed.AmiID,
1012: 		"auth_type":   ec2AuthType,
1013: 	}); err != nil {
1014: 		b.Logger().Warn("unable to set alias metadata", "err", err)
